qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Browser Control API Authentication Bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: this finding concerns unauthenticated /browser-control/* routes mounted before dashboard authentication, not missing CSRF protection on POST /apps/:appId/agent.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Development Mode Authentication Bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding requires the --dev deployment mode disabling authentication and exposing /parse-dashboard-config.json, not a CSRF gap on the agent endpoint.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: MasterKey Exposure via Configuration Endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different endpoint and root cause: this finding concerns authenticated disclosure of master keys from /parse-dashboard-config.json, not cross-site request submission to /apps/:appId/agent.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Client-Controlled Permissions Override
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same broad endpoint family but different root cause: this finding is about trusting a client-supplied permissions object after authentication, while the advisory is the absence of CSRF protection allowing a victim browser to submit POST /apps/:appId/agent.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: missing CSRF protection on the AI Agent API endpoint POST /apps/:appId/agent. The only qualifying finding involving the agent endpoint describes a server-side authorization flaw in client-controlled permissions, not CSRF middleware/token validation.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-3534-xp88-25rc describes missing CSRF protection on POST /apps/:appId/agent and a patch adding CSRF middleware plus a dashboard CSRF token.
permissions_override/vulnerability_report.json identifies POST /apps/:appId/agent but attributes the flaw to req.body.permissions being trusted for authorization decisions.
permissions_override/reproduction/poc_exploit.py obtains and sends a CSRF token, so its exploit path does not demonstrate absent CSRF protection.
The remaining qualifying findings target /browser-control/*, --dev authentication bypass, and /parse-dashboard-config.json master key disclosure.
